rationale_for_baseline_tier: A pointing abscess with surrounding cellulitis and early systemic features needs urgent same-day in-person drainage plus antibiotics — the procedure cannot be done remotely — without current sepsis, so T3 with escalation for systemic deterioration.

escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same-day in-person assessment for incision & drainage
escalation_edges.rationale: Definitive treatment (drainage) needs a clinician today.
escalation_edges.communication_template: This lump needs draining by a clinician today — please attend an urgent care clinic or GP that can do a minor procedure.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Spreading redness down the leg, fever >39°C, confusion, or unable to keep fluids/antibiotics down
escalation_edges.rationale: Signals systemic sepsis needing IV antibiotics/admission.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the redness spreads down your leg, your fever climbs above 39°C, you feel confused, or can't keep fluids down — go to the emergency department.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Ensure the patient can attend for drainage and has a low threshold to seek help if deteriorating overnight.
Choose MRSA-active antibiotics, swab, and consider decolonisation for recurrence.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This boil needs draining by a clinician today, plus antibiotics that cover MRSA. Don't squeeze it yourself. Watch for spreading redness, high fever or confusion.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Same-day GP/urgent care for drainage; ED/000 for sepsis
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Use an after-hours GP/urgent care that can drain it; ED for systemic symptoms.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document a pointing thigh furuncle/abscess with cellulitis and MRSA history; drainage performed/needed, MRSA-aware antibiotics and swab, decolonisation review.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Home antibiotics only with no drainage and no sepsis screen.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: The abscess progresses to sepsis, or MRSA is inadequately covered and the infection worsens.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine handling that delays drainage of a mature abscess with systemic features.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Calling an ambulance for an uncomplicated drainable abscess without sepsis.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot palpate/confirm fluctuance or drain remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Arrange urgent in-person drainage plus MRSA-aware antibiotics and safety-net for sepsis.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Managing remotely with antibiotics alone, ignoring MRSA, or advising self-drainage.
